questions.explanation: A set stores unique elements and provides average O(1) membership tests, making it ideal for unique labels and fast lookups.
questions.question: In Python, which data structure is best suited for storing unique class labels and performing fast membership checks (e.g., "is this label present?")?

questions.explanation: OpenCV images are typically BGR; cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB swaps channels to standard RGB ordering.
questions.question: In OpenCV, which color conversion code converts a BGR image (default OpenCV format) to RGB?

questions.explanation: Horizontal flipping simulates mirrored viewpoints and is a standard augmentation for many vision tasks.
questions.question: Which operation is a common data augmentation technique that helps a model become more robust to left-right viewpoint changes in images?

questions.explanation: model.eval() sets modules such as Dropout and BatchNorm to evaluation behavior; torch.no_grad() only disables gradient tracking.
questions.question: In PyTorch, which method switches a model to inference mode so layers like Dropout and BatchNorm behave deterministically?

questions.explanation: Homogeneous coordinates (x, y, 1) allow translation to be represented as matrix multiplication using a 3x3 transform.

options.A: A 2x2 matrix multiplied by a 2D point (x, y)
options.B: A 3x3 matrix multiplied by a 3D point (x, y, z)
options.C: A 3x3 matrix multiplied by a 2D point (x, y)
options.D: A 3x3 matrix multiplied by a homogeneous point (x, y, 1)

questions.question: You apply a 2D affine transform to an image. Which representation correctly uses homogeneous coordinates to apply translation along with rotation/scale?

questions.explanation: CrossEntropyLoss expects class indices as targets and raw, unnormalized logits; it applies log-softmax internally for multi-class classification per pixel.
questions.question: For multi-class semantic segmentation with integer class IDs per pixel (0..K-1), which PyTorch loss is typically appropriate when the model outputs raw logits of shape [N, K, H, W]?

questions.explanation: Transformers operate on sequences; patching and embedding turns the image into a token sequence that self-attention can process.

options.A: To compute edge maps before attention
options.B: To convert the image into a sequence of tokens suitable for transformer attention
options.C: To reduce the number of channels to 1 (grayscale)
options.D: To enforce translation invariance like max pooling

questions.question: In a Vision Transformer (ViT), what is the primary purpose of splitting an image into fixed-size patches and linearly embedding them?

questions.explanation: COCO mAP averages AP across a range of IoU thresholds (0.50:0.05:0.95), making it stricter than AP@0.5 alone.

options.A: mAP is computed only at IoU = 0.5
options.B: mAP is computed only at IoU = 0.75
options.C: mAP averages AP over multiple IoU thresholds (typically 0.50 to 0.95 in steps of 0.05)
options.D: mAP ignores IoU and uses only classification accuracy

questions.question: You are evaluating an object detector using COCO-style mAP. Which statement best describes how IoU thresholds are used in COCO mAP computation?

questions.explanation: Dice-based losses (often combined with BCE) are designed to handle imbalance by optimizing overlap, giving more signal to small foreground regions.

options.A: Replace ReLU with Tanh in all layers
options.B: Use a loss like Dice loss (or BCE+Dice) that emphasizes overlap on the foreground
options.C: Increase image resolution without changing anything else

questions.question: You train a U-Net for binary segmentation and observe severe class imbalance (foreground is ~2% of pixels). Which change is most directly targeted to improve learning on the minority class?

questions.explanation: TensorRT optimizes inference by fusing layers, selecting efficient kernels, and enabling reduced precision (FP16/INT8) to improve throughput and latency on GPUs.

options.A: Replacing convolution with FFT-based filtering for all kernels
options.B: Automatic mixed precision (e.g., FP16/INT8) and layer fusion to reduce memory bandwidth and latency
options.C: Converting the model to run only on CPU with vectorized NumPy
options.D: Training-time data augmentation to improve generalization

questions.question: You export a PyTorch model to ONNX and run it with TensorRT for faster inference on an NVIDIA GPU. Which optimization is TensorRT most known for applying during engine building?
